justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly called out ('The spec conflates these contexts' and escalations address cross-context coordination). Vehicle class constraint correctly marked invariant:true (BR-1), stop duration correctly marked invariant:false (BR-7/BR-8). 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided. All 3 recalculation triggers enumerated (BR-3: new delivery, BR-4: failed delivery, BR-5: driver deviation).

observations.text: Exceptional bounded context identification — 4 contexts with clear ownership descriptions
10 acceptance criteria far exceeds the minimum 4, covering happy paths and edge cases
10 edge cases identified with expected behavior — very thorough
5 detailed escalations with options and recommendations for each ambiguity
Rich ubiquitous language glossary with 11 terms precisely defined
BR-9 (zone uniqueness) and BR-10 (pending-only assignment) are valuable invariants beyond what the rubric required

Route recalculation triggers (BR-3, BR-4, BR-5) marked invariant:false — the rubric expected invariant:true since 'system must always recalculate on these events'. Bird's justification (optimization policy, not physical constraint) is defensible but diverges from rubric expectation.
Cross-context dependency between route recalculation and delivery lifecycle is surfaced in escalations and bounded context analysis
Business impact analysis covers financial, operational, user, and risk dimensions with specific stakeholder impacts
